description: Fix SDK fetch utility return type signatures and code examples that omit the `| undefined` return for 204 No Content responses. Six fetch functions document `Promise<T>` but the actual SDK returns `Promise<T | undefined>`. Code examples in tools, resources, and prompts pages access the return value without null checks.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix fetch utility return type signatures in utilities.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the SDK utilities docs to show the correct return types so that I handle 204 No Content responses properly.
acceptanceCriteria: The unchecked overload signature for `fetchJSON` shows `Promise<T | undefined>` (not `Promise<T>`)
The unchecked overload signature for `postJSON` shows `Promise<T | undefined>`
The unchecked overload signature for `postForm` shows `Promise<T | undefined>`
The unchecked overload signature for `postFormData` shows `Promise<T | undefined>`
The unchecked overload signature for `putJSON` shows `Promise<T | undefined>`
The unchecked overload signature for `patchJSON` shows `Promise<T | undefined>`
The checked (Zod schema) overloads still show `Promise<T>` (those are correct as-is since Zod parsing rejects undefined)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: Edit `docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx`. The fetch utility signatures are in the Fetch Utilities section. Each function has two overloads: one that accepts a Zod schema (returns `Promise<T>` ‚Äî correct) and one without (returns `Promise<T | undefined>` ‚Äî this is what needs fixing). Compare against `platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts` for the exact signatures. The `deleteJSON` unchecked overload already correctly shows `Promise<T | undefined>` in the docs ‚Äî match that pattern for the other six.

userStories.title: Fix code examples using fetchJSON without null checks
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the code examples in SDK docs to handle the `| undefined` return from `fetchJSON` so that I write correct code when following the examples.
acceptanceCriteria: The tool example in tools.mdx that uses `fetchJSON` for a POST request is updated to use `postJSON` instead (the dedicated function for POST requests), and handles the `| undefined` return
The resource example in resources.mdx that calls `fetchJSON` and accesses `.channels` handles the case where the return could be `undefined`
The prompt example in prompts.mdx that calls `fetchJSON` and accesses `.messages` handles the case where the return could be `undefined`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit three files:
1. `docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx` ‚Äî around line 134-145, the example uses `fetchJSON` with `{ method: 'POST', ... }` init for a POST request. Replace with `postJSON(url, body)` which is the idiomatic SDK function for POST. Add a null check or non-null assertion with comment.
2. `docs/content/docs/sdk/resources.mdx` ‚Äî around line 183-185, the example calls `fetchJSON<{ channels: ... }>(url)` then accesses `data.channels` without a check. Add a guard (e.g., `if (!data) return ...` or use the Zod-checked overload).
3. `docs/content/docs/sdk/prompts.mdx` ‚Äî around line 221-223, same pattern. The example calls `fetchJSON<{ messages: ... }>(url)` then accesses `data.messages`. Add a guard.

The simplest fix for the resource/prompt examples is to use the Zod-checked overload (pass a schema as third arg) which guarantees `T` ‚Äî this is actually the recommended pattern. Alternatively, add a null check.
